items.title: Intel swipes Qualcomm veteran of 25 years to lead client computing — Alex Katouzian jumps ship to oversee consumer CPUs and physical AI
items.description: Intel appoints Alex Katouzian to lead client computing and physical AI efforts and confirms Pushkar Ranade as CTO, signaling a deeper shift toward AI-driven and edge computing systems
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Intel has announced a pair of high-profile leadership appointments as it doubles down on its AI ambitions. According to an official press release today, the company is bringing in industry veteran Alex Katouzian to lead its client computing and emerging “physical AI” efforts, while Pushkar Ranade is coming in as Chief Technology Officer to steer the company’s long-term innovation strategy. The appointments clearly signal Intel's intent to more tightly align its core computing business with the rapidly evolving AI landscape, particularly at the edge and in real-world systems.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowAlex Katouzian is joining as executive vice president and general manager of the Client Computing and Physical AI Group, a role Intel says will align its traditional PC-focused business with emerging AI-driven systems spanning robotics, autonomous machines, and edge devices.Katouzian is bringing 25 years of industry experience from Qualcomm, spanning from when he joined as a senior engineer in 2002 to his last very senior role as executive vice president and group general manager of mobile, compute, and extended reality (XR).Article continues below You may like Intel Foundry boss leaves for Qualcomm ‘CPUs are cool again,' Intel and AMD reporting spikes in CPU demand due to agentic AI Intel Chairman Frank Yeary retires, Craig Barrat to become the new chairman of the Board of Directors He is widely regarded as a key figure in scaling Qualcomm’s mobile and compute platforms globally and is responsible for significant contributions to the industry, including the expansion of Snapdragon mobile platforms and Qualcomm’s push into PC and XR computing. Intel believes this experience will prove invaluable as the company moves to expand beyond traditional PCs into AI-enabled, connected, and edge-based computing ecosystems.Speaking on the appointment, which officially kicks off this month, Katouzian expressed excitement about joining Intel at what he described as a pivotal moment for AI-driven transformation across computing platforms.“Intel is creating the foundation for AI-driven transformation, from leading in AI PCs, to scaling AI inference at the edge, and accelerating the future of physical AI systems,” wrote Katouzian. “I’m excited to join Lip-Bu and the Intel team at this critical moment to help scale innovation and deliver the next generation of computing experiences.” Katouzian announced his departure from Qualcomm on LinkedIn.The second appointment sees Pushkar Ranade transition to permanent Chief Technology Officer after serving in the role on an interim basis for several months. Ranade is not a new face at Intel and has been with the company for over 10 years.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsAccording to Intel, his role will involve leading the company’s technology strategy, overseeing special technology initiatives, and driving development across emerging areas such as quantum computing, neuromorphic computing, photonics, and advanced materials. He will also serve as chief of staff to the company’s CEO.Both appointments continue an existing trend as more tech companies pivot to a stronger AI focus. Microsoft, for instance, created a dedicated AI division led by DeepMind co-founder Mustafa Suleyman, alongside a series of follow-on hires to strengthen its AI leadership. Google has also brought in senior robotics leadership to push AI into physical systems. items.titleZh: 英特尔挖走高通25年老将领导客户端计算业务 — 亚历克斯·卡图齐安跳槽负责消费级CPU和物理AI
items.descriptionZh:
英特尔宣布两项重磅高管任命，加速人工智能战略布局。这家芯片巨头正在引入行业资深人士Alex Katouzian领导其客户端计算与“物理AI”业务，同时任命Pushkar Ranade为首席技术官，负责长期创新战略。这些举动清晰地表明，英特尔正试图将核心计算业务与快速演进的AI生态——尤其是边缘计算和实体系统——更紧密地绑定。

Alex Katouzian将出任执行副总裁兼客户端计算与物理AI集团总经理，这个新设立的职位旨在将英特尔传统的PC业务与新兴的AI驱动系统（涵盖机器人、自主机器和边缘设备）对齐。Katouzian拥有25年行业经验，其中大部分在高通度过：他于2002年以高级工程师身份加入，最后担任的是移动、计算和扩展现实（XR）部门的执行副总裁兼集团总经理。他在全球范围内推动了骁龙移动平台的扩展，并领导高通进军PC和XR计算领域，被公认为对移动和计算平台规模化有重大贡献的关键人物。英特尔相信，这些经验对于公司从传统PC向AI增强、互联和边缘计算生态扩张将极为宝贵。Katouzian在正式上任时表示：“英特尔正在为AI驱动的转型奠定基础——从引领AI PC，到规模化边缘AI推理，再到加速物理AI系统的未来。我很高兴在关键时刻加入Intel团队，帮助扩展创新并交付下一代计算体验。”

第二项任命是Pushkar Ranade从临时首席技术官正式转正。他已在英特尔工作超过10年，并非新面孔。作为CTO，他将领导公司的技术战略，监督特殊技术倡议，并推动量子计算、神经形态计算、光子学、先进材料等新兴领域的开发。同时，他还将担任CEO的幕僚长。

这两项人事任命延续了科技公司向AI转型的大趋势。例如，微软成立了由DeepMind联合创始人Mustafa Suleyman领导的独立AI部门，并持续强化AI领导团队；谷歌也引入了高级机器人领域领导人，推动AI进入物理系统。英特尔通过引入高通级别的移动和计算平台专家，以及从内部提拔长期技术高管，试图在AI PC、边缘推理和物理AI三个战场上同时发力，为其芯片业务寻找新的增长引擎。

history.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
history.description:
Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of abandoning its founding mission of developing AI to benefit humanity and shifting focus to boosting profits instead. Elon Musk took the stand on April 28th as the first witness called, portraying his interest in founding OpenAI as an effort to help save humanity across three days of testimony, before his financial manager and Neuralink CEO, Jared Birchall, took the stand.
Week two of the trial is underway, with professor Stuart Russell and OpenAI cofounder Greg Brockman taking the stand on Monday, as the proceedings also added a live audio stream on YouTube.

On Tuesday, Brockman’s testimony continued, with Shivon Zilis, a former OpenAI board member who shares four children with Musk, up next. It’s possible that audio of her testimony will not be available on the stream, with lawyers citing threats against her and her children. Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella is scheduled to appear on Monday, with OpenAI cofounder and former chief scientist Ilya Sutskever lined up to testify after that.
Musk was a cofounder of OpenAI and claims that Altman and Brockman tricked him into giving the company money, only to turn their backs on their original goal. However, OpenAI says that “This lawsuit has always been a baseless and jealous bid to derail a competitor” in a bid to boost Musk’s own SpaceX / xAI / X companies that have launched Grok as a competitor to ChatGPT.
In his lawsuit, Musk is asking for the removal of Altman and Brockman, and for OpenAI to stop operating as a public benefit corporation. Musk has also demanded that OpenAI’s nonprofit receive up to $150 billion in damages he’s asking for if he wins the case.
Here’s all the latest on the trial between Musk and Altman:

history.content: Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of abandoning its founding mission of developing AI to benefit humanity and shifting focus to boosting profits instead. Elon Musk took the stand on April 28th as the first witness called, portraying his interest in founding OpenAI as an effort to help save humanity across three days of testimony, before his financial manager and Neuralink CEO, Jared Birchall, took the stand.Week two of the trial is underway, with professor Stuart Russell and OpenAI cofounder Greg Brockman taking the stand on Monday, as the proceedings also added a live audio stream on YouTube.On Tuesday, Brockman’s testimony continued, with Shivon Zilis, a former OpenAI board member who shares four children with Musk, up next. It’s possible that audio of her testimony will not be available on the stream, with lawyers citing threats against her and her children. Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella is scheduled to appear on Monday, with OpenAI cofounder and former chief scientist Ilya Sutskever lined up to testify after that.Musk was a cofounder of OpenAI and claims that Altman and Brockman tricked him into giving the company money, only to turn their backs on their original goal. However, OpenAI says that “This lawsuit has always been a baseless and jealous bid to derail a competitor” in a bid to boost Musk’s own SpaceX / xAI / X companies that have launched Grok as a competitor to ChatGPT.In his lawsuit, Musk is asking for the removal of Altman and Brockman, and for OpenAI to stop operating as a public benefit corporation. Musk has also demanded that OpenAI’s nonprofit receive up to $150 billion in damages he’s asking for if he wins the case.Here’s all the latest on the trial between Musk and Altman:

history.descriptionZh:
奥特曼与马斯克之间的高 stakes 审判正在重塑 OpenAI 及 ChatGPT 的未来。2024 年，马斯克提起诉讼，指控 OpenAI 背离了其创立时“开发人工智能以造福人类”的使命，转而专注于追求利润。4 月 28 日，马斯克作为首位证人出庭，连续三天作证，阐述自己参与创立 OpenAI 的初衷是“拯救人类”。随后，他的财务经理兼 Neuralink 首席执行官贾里德·伯查尔出庭作证。

审判进入第二周，加州大学伯克利分校教授斯图尔特·罗素和 OpenAI 联合创始人格雷格·布罗克曼于周一（5 月 5 日）出庭，庭审过程通过 YouTube 进行实时音频直播。周二，布罗克曼的证词继续，前 OpenAI 董事会成员、与马斯克育有四个孩子的希冯·齐利斯将随后作证。由于律师引用了针对她及其子女的威胁，齐利斯的证词音频可能不会在直播中提供。微软首席执行官萨提亚·纳德拉定于下周一（5 月 12 日）出庭，OpenAI 联合创始人、前首席科学家伊利亚·苏茨克维也在证人名单上。

马斯克是 OpenAI 的联合创始人之一，他声称奥特曼和布罗克曼诱骗他投入资金，最终却背弃了最初的目标。对此，OpenAI 回应称：“这起诉讼一直是毫无根据且出于嫉妒的企图，旨在阻碍竞争对手”，并指责马斯克试图借此为其旗下的 SpaceX、xAI 和 X 公司谋利——这些公司推出了与 ChatGPT 竞争的 Grok 聊天机器人。

在诉讼中，马斯克要求撤换奥特曼和布罗克曼，并责令 OpenAI 停止作为公益公司运营。此外，如果胜诉，他要求 OpenAI 的非营利部分向他支付高达 1500 亿美元的赔偿金。

以下是关于马斯克与奥特曼审判的最新进展。

history.title: NVIDIA and ServiceNow Partner on New Autonomous AI Agents for Enterprises
history.description: Enterprise AI has learned to generate. It has learned to reason. Now companies are asking the next question: How should AI act? Early agent systems have shown what’s possible, moving beyond simple prompts to take on more complex tasks. The next step is bringing those capabilities into enterprise environments — where agents must operate with […]
history.content: Enterprise AI has learned to generate. It has learned to reason. Now companies are asking the next question: How should AI act? Early agent systems have shown what’s possible, moving beyond simple prompts to take on more complex tasks. The next step is bringing those capabilities into enterprise environments — where agents must operate with context, control and consistency across real workflows. At ServiceNow Knowledge 2026, NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang joined ServiceNow chairman and CEO Bill McDermott during the opening keynote to discuss the next phase of enterprise AI. The companies are expanding their collaboration across the full stack, delivering specialized autonomous AI agents that are safe and easy to adopt — powered by NVIDIA accelerated computing, open models, domain-specific skills and secure agent execution software, and bringing together enterprise workflow context from ServiceNow Action Fabric and governance from ServiceNow AI Control Tower. ServiceNow is introducing Project Arc, a long-running, self-evolving autonomous desktop agent designed for knowledge workers, including developers, IT teams and administrators. Unlike standalone AI agents, Project Arc connects natively to the ServiceNow AI Platform through ServiceNow Action Fabric to bring governance, auditability and workflow intelligence to every action the autonomous desktop agent takes. It can access the local file systems, terminals and applications installed on a machine to complete complex, multistep tasks that traditional automation can’t handle, but with the controls enterprises actually need to deploy AI at scale. The work is designed based on three requirements every company will need for long-running, autonomous agents: open models and domain-specific skills that can be customized and security that helps agents act without exposing sensitive data or systems — all running on AI factories that deliver efficient tokenomics. Bringing this level of autonomy to enterprises requires control from the start. Project Arc uses NVIDIA OpenShell, an open source secure runtime for developing and deploying autonomous agents in sandboxed, policy-governed environments. ServiceNow is building on and contributing to OpenShell to advance a common foundation for secure, enterprise-grade agent execution. With OpenShell, enterprises can define what an agent can see, which tools it can use and how each action is contained. “Project Arc represents the next step in our ongoing collaboration with NVIDIA, bringing autonomous execution to the desktop,” said Jon Sigler, executive vice president and general manager of AI Platform at ServiceNow. “By combining OpenShell’s runtime layer with ServiceNow AI Control Tower, and powered by ServiceNow Action Fabric, we’re delivering the governance and security that enterprise AI requires.” Open Models and Agent Skills Scale Enterprise AI To be effective, enterprise AI systems must be adaptable. NVIDIA and ServiceNow are building on an open ecosystem that allows organizations to tailor models and applications to their specific domains and data. NVIDIA agent skills enable specialized agents, such as ServiceNow AI Specialists, to deliver targeted capabilities across enterprise workflows. For example, the NVIDIA AI-Q Blueprint for building specialized deep research agents empowers ServiceNow AI Specialists to gather context, synthesize information and support more complex decision-making across business functions. In addition, the NVIDIA Agent Toolkit, including NVIDIA Nemotron open models, provide flexible building blocks and specialized skills for developing customized AI applications. To support real-world performance that these systems can perform reliably, the companies are also advancing NOWAI-Bench, an open benchmarking suite for enterprise AI agents, integrated with the NVIDIA NeMo Gym library. NOWAI-Bench includes EnterpriseOps-Gym, one of the industry’s most challenging enterprise agent benchmarks, where Nemotron 3 Super currently ranks No. 1 among open source models. Unlike general benchmarks, these evaluations focus on multistep workflows — where enterprise AI systems often encounter real challenges — helping teams build agents that perform reliably in production environments. Efficient AI Factories As AI agents become long running and always on, scaling them across millions of workflows requires not just capability but efficiency — making token economics central to enterprise AI. NVIDIA AI factories are built to deliver the lowest-cost, most-efficient tokenomics for production AI. The NVIDIA Blackwell platform delivers more than 50x greater token output per watt than NVIDIA Hopper, resulting in nearly 35x lower cost per million tokens. For enterprises running agents across millions of workflows, that efficiency can determine how quickly AI moves from pilots to broad production use. ServiceNow AI Control Tower integrates with the NVIDIA Enterprise AI Factory validated design, extending governance and observability to large-scale AI workloads. With added agent observability capabilities, organizations can monitor behavior in real time and manage AI systems across their full lifecycle — from deployment to optimization. AI is becoming a new way that work gets done. What’s changing now is that the core pieces required to deploy it at scale — capable agents, built-in guardrails and proven performance — are all coming together. The companies that move fastest will be the ones that give agents the infrastructure to act, the context to make decisions and the governance to keep every action accountable — and NVIDIA and ServiceNow are making this a reality for the world’s enterprises. Learn more about NVIDIA OpenShell and the NVIDIA AI-Q Blueprint.
history.author: Kari Briski
history.titleZh: NVIDIA与ServiceNow合作开发面向企业的新型自主AI代理
history.descriptionZh:
在企业AI的演进中，机器已经从学会生成和推理，进化到需要“行动”的阶段。早期的AI代理系统已展现出超越简单提示词、处理更复杂任务的能力，但下一步是将这些能力带入企业环境——代理必须在真实工作流中，具备上下文、受控且一致地运作。在ServiceNow Knowledge 2026大会上，NVIDIA创始人兼CEO黄仁勋与ServiceNow董事长兼CEO Bill McDermott在开幕主题演讲中探讨了企业AI的下一阶段。两家公司正扩大全栈合作，交付专用、安全且易于采用的自主AI代理——这些代理由NVIDIA加速计算、开放模型、领域特定技能和安全代理执行软件驱动，并整合了ServiceNow Action Fabric的企业工作流上下文以及ServiceNow AI Control Tower的治理能力。

ServiceNow发布了Project Arc——一个专为知识工作者（包括开发者、IT团队和系统管理员）设计的长期运行、自我进化的自主桌面代理。与独立AI代理不同，Project Arc通过ServiceNow Action Fabric原生连接到ServiceNow AI平台，为自主桌面代理的每一次行动带来治理、可审计性和工作流智能。它可以访问本地文件系统、终端和机器上安装的应用程序，从而完成传统自动化无法处理的复杂多步任务，但同时又具备企业大规模部署AI所需的控制机制。该项目基于每个公司在长期运行、自主代理方面都需要的三个核心要求设计：可定制化的开放模型和领域特定技能、帮助代理在不暴露敏感数据或系统的情况下行动的安全性——所有这些都运行在提供高效token经济学的AI工厂之上。

将这种程度的自主性带入企业，需要从一开始就进行控制。Project Arc使用NVIDIA OpenShell——一个开源安全运行时，用于在沙盒化、策略管控的环境中开发和部署自主代理。ServiceNow正在基于OpenShell进行构建并为其贡献力量，以推进企业级代理执行的安全通用基础。通过OpenShell，企业可以定义代理能看到什么、能使用哪些工具以及每个行动如何被隔离。ServiceNow AI平台执行副总裁兼总经理Jon Sigler表示：“Project Arc代表了我们与NVIDIA持续合作的下一步，将自主执行带到桌面。通过将OpenShell的运行时层与ServiceNow AI Control Tower相结合，并依托ServiceNow Action Fabric，我们为企业AI提供了所需的治理和安全性。”

开放模型和代理技能正在为企业AI规模化提供支撑。为了有效运作，企业AI系统必须可适应。NVIDIA和ServiceNow正构建于开放生态系统之上，允许组织根据自身特定领域和数据定制模型和应用。NVIDIA代理技能使专门的代理（如ServiceNow AI Specialists）能够在企业工作流中提供有针对性的能力。例如，用于构建专门深度研究代理的NVIDIA AI-Q蓝图，使ServiceNow AI Specialists能够跨业务功能收集上下文、综合信息并支持更复杂的决策。此外，NVIDIA Agent Toolkit（包括NVIDIA Nemotron开放模型）为开发定制化AI应用提供了灵活的构建块和专业技能。为了支持这些系统的可靠真实性能，两家公司还推进了NOWAI-Bench——一个面向企业AI代理的开放基准测试套件，与NVIDIA NeMo Gym库集成。NOWAI-Bench包含EnterpriseOps-Gym，这是行业中最具挑战性的企业代理基准之一，目前Nemotron 3 Super在开源模型中排名第一。与通用基准不同，这些评估专注于多步工作流——企业AI系统经常在此遇到真正挑战——帮助团队构建在生产环境中可靠执行的代理。

高效的AI工厂是关键。随着AI代理变得长期运行并始终在线，跨数百万个工作流进行扩展不仅需要能力，还需要效率——这使得token经济学成为企业AI的核心。NVIDIA AI工厂专为生产级AI提供最低成本、最高效的token经济学而设计。NVIDIA Blackwell平台每瓦token输出比NVIDIA Hopper高出50倍以上，使得每百万token的成本降低近35倍。对于跨数百万个工作流运行代理的企业来说，这种效率决定了AI从试点到大规模生产的速度。ServiceNow AI Control Tower与NVIDIA企业AI工厂验证设计集成，将治理和可观察性扩展到大规模AI工作负载。通过新增的代理可观察性能力，组织可以实时监控行为，并管理AI系统从部署到优化的全生命周期。

AI正在成为一种全新的工作方式。当前的变化是，大规模部署所需的核心部件——能力齐全的代理、内置护栏和经过验证的性能——正在汇聚在一起。行动最快的公司将是为代理提供行动基础设施、决策上下文以及使每个行动都可问责的治理机制的公司——而NVIDIA和ServiceNow正在让这一切在全球企业成为现实。了解更多信息，请访问NVIDIA OpenShell和NVIDIA AI-Q蓝图。

history.content: OpenAI’s first hardware product might be a phone instead of a mysterious Jony Ive gadget. As reported by MacRumors, supply chain analyst Ming-Chi Kuo shared details about the rumored phone, claiming OpenAI is “fast-tracking” it and aiming to start mass production in early 2027.According to Kuo, the phone will run on a “customized version of the [MediaTek] Dimensity 9600,” which is expected to launch this fall and follow up the Dimensity 9500 currently powering phones like the Vivo X300 Pro and the Oppo Find X9 Pro.The custom chip’s “headline spec” will be its image signal processor (ISP), which will have “enhanced HDR” that Kuo says will improve the phone’s real-world visual sensing capabilities. The OpenAI phone could also include LPDDR6 memory, UFS 5.0 storage, and a “dual-NPU architecture” for running different kinds of AI computation simultaneously, like language and vision tasks.Kuo also states that the phone’s “combined 2027–2028 shipments could reach around 30 million units.” That would put the OpenAI phone’s sales near those of a typical Samsung flagship — a lofty goal for the company’s first hardware product.
history.author: Stevie Bonifield
history.titleZh: 据报道，OpenAI将推出一款ChatGPT手机
history.descriptionZh:
OpenAI的首款硬件产品可能并非此前传闻中与苹果前设计总监Jony Ive合作的神秘设备，而是一款手机。据MacRumors报道，知名供应链分析师郭明錤（Ming-Chi Kuo）分享了对这款传闻中手机的详细分析，称OpenAI正在“加速推进”该项目，目标是在2027年初开始量产。

根据郭明錤的说法，这款手机将搭载“定制版联发科天玑9600”芯片。天玑9600预计将于今年秋季发布，是天玑9500的后续产品，后者目前用于Vivo X300 Pro和Oppo Find X9 Pro等机型。该定制芯片的“核心规格”是其图像信号处理器（ISP），配备了“增强型HDR”功能，郭明錤表示这将显著提升手机在真实场景中的视觉感知能力。此外，OpenAI手机还可能采用LPDDR6内存、UFS 5.0存储，并引入“双NPU架构”设计——即同时搭载两枚神经网络处理单元，可并行处理不同类型的AI计算任务，例如同时运行语言模型和视觉模型。

郭明錤还预测，这款手机在2027至2028年间的总出货量可能达到约3000万部。这一数字接近三星旗舰机型的销量水平，对于一家公司的首款硬件产品来说，是一个相当雄心勃勃的目标。

从技术影响和应用场景来看，OpenAI手机的核心卖点在于其深度整合的AI能力。定制芯片的双NPU架构意味着设备可以同时高效运行语言理解和图像识别任务，例如实时翻译、视觉搜索、增强现实交互等，而不需要在不同计算单元之间切换或降低性能。增强型HDR ISP则进一步优化了摄像头对现实场景的光学信息捕捉能力，可能为AI驱动的拍照、物体识别和环境感知提供更高质量的原始数据。这类硬件创新有望将AI从“云端依赖”进一步推向“端侧实时处理”，使手机成为真正意义上的个人AI助手终端。

尽管目前该产品的具体外观、操作系统（是否会深度集成OpenAI的ChatGPT或未来多模态模型）以及定价策略尚未披露，但郭明錤的分析表明，OpenAI正在试图通过定制化硬件与软件生态的深度耦合，直接进入竞争激烈的智能手机市场，与苹果、三星等巨头展开差异化竞争。如果进展顺利，这款手机将成为AI原生硬件的里程碑式产品。

history.title: Palantir co-founder Peter Thiel backs $140M wave-powered AI data center startup — Panthalassa aims to run offshore compute nodes using ocean energy
history.description: Panthalassa raises $140M to build wave-powered offshore nodes that generate electricity and run AI workloads at sea, tackling data center energy and cooling challenges.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter An official press release yesterday confirmed a $140 million investment to power AI data centers at sea. The funding goes to Pathanlassa — a renewable energy and ocean technology company — and was raised in a Series B financing led by avid investor and former PayPal CEO and Palantir co-founder Peter Thiel, alongside several other investors.The Portland-based start-up said it would use the funding to complete its pilot manufacturing facility in Oregon for its Ocean-3 series nodes — an autonomous platform that will house and power data centers for AI inference computing at sea, using electricity generated from ocean waves.“There are three sources of energy on the planet with tens of terawatts of new capacity potential: solar, nuclear, and the open ocean,” said Garth Sheldon-Coulson, Co-Founder and CEO of Panthalassa. “We’ve built a technology platform that operates in the planet’s most energy-dense wave regions, far from shore, and turns that resource into reliable, clean power. We’re now ready to build factories, deploy fleets, and provide a sustainable new source of energy for humanity.”Article continues below You may like US startup plans to build data centers inside ocean-based wind turbines, servers water cooled via chilly North Sea Amazon, Microsoft, and Google under investor pressure to disclose data center water and power use New AI data center in Utah will generate and consume more than twice the amount of power the entire state uses Panthalassa’s solution is an autonomous, fully integrated system called a node that houses both AI infrastructure and power-generating hardware in a single offshore unit. Each lollipop-shaped node consists of a buoyant spherical head connected to a long, submerged vertical tube and structural frame.As ocean waves pass, the node bobs up and down, but, crucially, the surrounding water moves only in small orbital paths. This relative motion between the structure and the water column induces oscillations within the tube, effectively driving seawater up and down through the system.That oscillating flow is channeled into the spherical chamber through a high-pressure jet, where it is converted into usable mechanical energy. The water then passes through internal turbines, generating electricity before recirculating back into the tube to repeat the cycle. The system is designed as a closed hydraulic loop that continuously extracts energy from wave-induced motion.As the ocean never stops moving, the system generates power around the clock. That power runs the onboard payloads. For truly autonomous operation, the system transmits data back to shore via satellite, eliminating the need for tethering. Another noteworthy benefit of the system is that the surrounding ocean provides free supercooling, solving one of the biggest engineering challenges in land-based data centers.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsEach node also incorporates propulsion and station-keeping systems, allowing it to maintain optimal positioning or operate as part of a distributed offshore network, effectively coupling renewable energy generation directly with modular AI compute at sea.After decades of research and development, and several preceding versions, Panthalassa says it plans to “deploy its Ocean-3 pilot node series in the northern Pacific Ocean, demonstrating AI inference capabilities and refining its manufacturing process in preparation for commercial deployments in 2027.”AI’s electricity demand is rising much faster than conventional power infrastructure can keep up with. In many regions, grid operators are already struggling to keep up, while communities are increasingly pushing back against new builds over concerns about land use, noise, and energy diversion. In response, companies are scrambling for alternatives, and the solutions are becoming increasingly unconventional. “The future demands more compute than we can imagine,” said Peter Thiel. “Extra-terrestrial solutions are no longer science fiction. Panthalassa has opened the ocean frontier.”Panthalassa’s wave-powered offshore compute nodes are the latest in a growing line of radical bets on how to power AI sustainably. Last month, we reported that Meta had signed a partnership with an energy start-up to beam solar energy from space to enable continuous, 24/7 power. Other startups are exploring the idea of moving data centers entirely off-planet. These are just a few of the many other initiatives to meet AI’s seemingly impossible electricity demands. history.author: Etiido Uko
history.titleZh: Palantir联合创始人彼得·蒂尔支持1.4亿美元波浪能人工智能数据中心初创公司——Panthalassa旨在利用海洋能源运行海上计算节点
history.descriptionZh:
美国初创公司Panthalassa获得1.4亿美元B轮融资，由彼得·蒂尔（Peter Thiel）领投，用于建造位于海洋中的AI数据中心。这些数据中心将利用波浪能自主发电，为AI推理计算提供清洁、可持续的电力。该公司计划在2027年实现商业部署，其Ocean-3系列节点将成为全球首个完全自主、离网的海上AI计算平台。

**背景：AI算力需求激增与能源瓶颈**

随着AI大模型和推理任务的爆发式增长，传统陆基数据中心的电力消耗已远超电网负荷。许多地区的电网运营商面临巨大压力，而新建数据中心也因土地占用、噪音污染和能源挤兑等问题遭到社区抵制。在此背景下，科技巨头和初创公司纷纷探索非常规能源方案——从太空太阳能到海上风电机组内置数据中心，Panthalassa的波浪能方案正是这一趋势的最新代表。彼得·蒂尔在声明中表示：“未来的计算需求超乎想象，地外解决方案不再是科幻，Panthalassa打开了海洋前沿。”

**核心技术：波浪能驱动的自治节点**

Panthalassa的核心产品是一个外形类似棒棒糖的自主平台，称为“节点”。每个节点由浮球状头部、长垂直管状结构和水下框架组成。其工作原理是利用海洋波浪的上下运动：当波浪经过时，节点整体上下浮动，但周围海水仅做小轨道运动，这种相对运动导致管内海水产生振荡流。振荡流通过高压喷嘴进入球状腔室，转化为机械能，再驱动内部涡轮机发电。海水随后回流到管内，形成闭合液压回路，持续从波浪运动中提取能量。由于海洋永不静止，系统可全天候发电。

每个节点内部集成了AI推理计算所需的服务器硬件和发电设备，完全自给自足。数据通过卫星传回陆地，无需海底电缆。此外，周围海水提供了天然的超冷却能力，解决了陆基数据中心最大的热管理难题。节点还配备推进和定位系统，可自主保持最优位置，并能组成分布式海上计算网络。

**性能参数与对比**

Panthalassa未披露单个节点的具体算力和发电功率，但强调其选址于全球能量密度最高的波浪区域（如北太平洋），远离海岸，能稳定输出清洁电力。与传统陆基数据中心相比，其优势在于无需依赖电网、无需大规模水冷系统、无需土地占用；与海上风电相比，波浪能装置更紧凑、更适应深海环境。该公司经过数十年研发和多代原型验证，现计划在俄勒冈州建设试点制造工厂，并于2027年在北太平洋部署首批Ocean-3系列试点节点，用于演示AI推理能力并优化制造流程。

**技术影响与应用场景**

该方案直击AI算力扩张的核心痛点——能源供应与环保压力。对于需要大量低延迟推理计算的应用（如自动驾驶、工业机器人、实时视频分析），海上节点可部署在靠近海洋经济活动区域（如航运航线、海上油气平台、海洋观测站），提供就近算力。长远看，这为全球AI基础设施的布局提供了全新范式：从陆地向海洋迁移，规避陆地资源竞争和环境法规限制。Panthalassa联合创始人兼CEO Garth Sheldon-Coulson表示：“地球上只有三种能源拥有数十太瓦级新增潜力：太阳能、核能和开阔海洋。我们的平台能将这些资源转化为可靠清洁的电力，现在准备建厂、部署舰队，为人类提供可持续的新能源。”

尽管波浪能技术的规模化成本、极端天气下的可靠性以及海洋生态影响仍有待验证，但Panthalassa已获得彼得·蒂尔等知名投资者的背书，成为AI能源领域最激进的创新之一。随着今年Meta宣布与初创公司合作从太空传输太阳能、其他公司探索将数据中心迁入太空，Panthalassa的海上计算节点代表着AI行业正不遗余力地寻找“不可能”的电力解决方案。

history.title: Huawei braces for $12 billion in AI chip revenue driven by homegrown AI model demand — Chinese fabs can barely keep up as Nvidia's market share craters within the region
history.description: The projection, based on orders already received from major Chinese technology firms including Alibaba, ByteDance, and Tencent, would represent growth of at least 60% year-over-year.
history.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Huawei expects revenue from its AI processors to reach roughly $12 billion in 2026, up from $7.5 billion last year. The projection, based on orders already received from major Chinese technology firms including Alibaba, ByteDance, and Tencent, would represent at least 60% year-over-year growth and position Huawei as the dominant supplier in a domestic AI chip market that Morgan Stanley estimates could reach $67 billion by 2030. The surge has coincided with Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang confirming that Nvidia's share of the Chinese AI accelerator market has collapsed to zero percent.These numbers describe a market that has bifurcated with unusual speed. Just 18 months ago, Nvidia supplied the vast majority of AI training and inference silicon used by Chinese cloud providers. Today, Huawei's Ascend 950PR is the primary procurement target for China's largest tech companies, and a training-focused successor named the 950DT is scheduled for Q4 this year.The impact of DeepSeek V4This raging demand can be largely attributed to the release of DeepSeek’s V4 LLM in April, which has been optimized specifically for Huawei's Ascend architecture and its CANN software framework rather than for Nvidia's CUDA ecosystem. Huawei engineers, per reporting from South China Morning Post, are said to have collaborated directly with DeepSeek ahead of the model’s launch, and the company confirmed that its full Ascend SuperNode product line was adapted for V4 inference on day one. Alibaba Cloud and Tencent Cloud both deployed V4 services within hours of release.Article continues below You may like Huawei could seize China’s AI chip crown in 2026 as Nvidia’s H200 shipments stall in regulatory limbo China's homegrown silicon suppliers gain traction as Nvidia struggles to get its chips into the market Chinese GPU maker Cambricon's Q1 revenue hits $423 million as country's homegrown AI chip market accelerates The 950PR is currently the only Chinese-made AI processor that supports FP8, a compressed numerical format that allows more operations per second and lowers per-query costs. V4 uses a Mixture-of-Experts architecture with up to 1 trillion total parameters but activates only around 37 billion per inference pass. That favors inference-efficient hardware, which plays to the 950PR's strengths over its limitations in raw training throughput.DeepSeek gave Huawei early optimization access, but didn’t extend the same to Nvidia or AMD. While V4's open weights are released in standard formats compatible with CUDA-based frameworks, DeepSeek's own infrastructure runs on Huawei Ascend silicon. The collaboration has pulled forward procurement timelines across the Chinese cloud industry, and chip prices for the 950PR have reportedly risen by about 20% as a result of the demand.SMIC capacity and production Huawei's ability to fill those orders depends on SMIC, China's leading foundry. SMIC manufactures the 950PR on its N+3 process, a 7nm-class node built without EUV lithography. Huawei is said to be targeting production of roughly 750,000 950PR units this year, with full-scale shipments expected in the second half following samples that were shipped to customers in January, but that figure is expected to fall short of demand.Meanwhile, SMIC has been working on expanding its advanced-node capacity for more than a year. The goal is a five-fold increase over a period of two years that’ll lift 7nm and 5nm production to 100,000 wafers per month and half a million by 2030. In addition, the combined capacity for 22nm and below could rise from 30,000-50,000 wafer starts per month in 2025 to 50,000-60,000 or higher this year. Huawei is adding two dedicated fabrication plants, though ownership structures remain unclear. Once fully operational, those facilities could exceed the current output of comparable lines at SMIC.Yields remain a thorn in China’s side, with SMIC’s 7nm-class process delivering substantially fewer good dies per wafer than TSMC’s equivalent nodes, and the 950PR is likely to be a much larger chip than a TSMC equivalent. SMIC’s cycle time from wafer start to finished and packaged as an Ascend processor is also a problem, currently sitting at around eight months, according to estimates from JP Morgan. For similar nodes at TSMC, it’s around three months.Then there’s HBM — Huawei announced in September that it had developed its own HBM chips with up to 1.6 TB/s bandwidth, HiBL 1.0, and HiZQ 2.0, in partnership with CXMT, but how quickly CXMT can ramp production of competitive HBM remains an open question.Nvidia's collapse in ChinaHuang's admission that “In China, we have now dropped to zero,” came during an interview with the Special Competitive Studies Project's "Memos to the President" podcast. He criticized U.S. export policy as having "already largely backfired," arguing that conceding a market the size of China doesn’t make strategic sense. What to read next Nvidia market share in China falls to less than 60% DeepSeek launches 1.6 trillion parameter V4 on Huawei chips as U.S. escalates AI theft accusations With H200s set to flow into China, Groq is reportedly set to follow soon after The H200, which Nvidia received U.S. licenses to sell to China earlier this year, hasn’t shipped a single unit despite receiving orders. Contradictory regulatory requirements from Washington and Beijing created a stalemate at customs: U.S. regulators require that H200 chips ordered by Chinese customers be used only inside China, while Beijing has instructed domestic technology companies to limit Nvidia hardware to overseas operations.Nvidia confirmed in its FY2026 10-K filing that it’s "effectively foreclosed from competing in China's data center computing market" and is not assuming any data center compute revenue from the region in its current outlook. Bernstein analysts estimated earlier this year that Nvidia’s share of the China AI GPU market could fall to roughly 8% in the coming years, down from 66% in 2024, both due to U.S. restrictions and because domestic vendors are being pushed to cover up to 80% of demand from domestic sources. TrendForce projected in December that China's high-end AI chip market would grow by more than 60% in 2026, with domestic suppliers capturing about half of the total.950PR performanceThe 950PR performs somewhere in between Nvidia’s H100 and H200, and outperforms the restricted H20 by an estimated factor of 2.8 times, but trails the H200 in both compute and memory bandwidth. That 2.8 figure can’t be verified, however, since Hopper-era hardware doesn’t support FP4 natively.Huawei compensates by linking large numbers of processors via optical interconnects. Its CloudMatrix 384 system combines twelve racks of Ascend modules into a 384-processor fabric delivering roughly 300 PFLOPS, though at nearly four times the power draw of Nvidia's comparable GB200-based configurations.The 950PR is primarily an inference chip, though; the training-focused 950DT, expected in Q4, is designed for deep learning workloads and could narrow the gap with Nvidia's Hopper generation for model training tasks. Until it ships, Chinese firms that need to train large foundation models domestically face constraints that inference silicon can’t fully solve.As for Huawei's CANN software ecosystem, it’s now thought to have more than four million developers, but it remains far smaller than Nvidia's CUDA install base. Whether CANN can attract enough third-party development to become self-sustaining remains to be seen. For now, commercial momentum is running in Huawei's favor inside China, driven by the simple absence of alternatives.
history.author: Luke James
history.titleZh: 华为准备迎接120亿美元AI芯片收入，本土AI模型需求驱动——中国晶圆厂几乎跟不上，英伟达在该地区市场份额暴跌
history.descriptionZh:
华为预计其AI处理器业务收入将从去年的75亿美元增长至2026年的约120亿美元，这一预测基于已从阿里巴巴、字节跳动和腾讯等中国主要科技公司收到的订单。如果实现，这将代表至少60%的同比增长，并使华为成为中国本土AI芯片市场的主导供应商。摩根士丹利估计，到2030年该市场规模可能达到670亿美元。与此同时，英伟达CEO黄仁勋确认，英伟达在中国AI加速器市场的份额已降至零。

这些数字描述了一个以异常速度分化的市场。就在18个月前，英伟达还为中国云服务商提供绝大多数AI训练和推理芯片。如今，华为的Ascend 950PR已成为中国大型科技公司的首要采购目标，而一款面向训练的继任型号950DT计划于今年第四季度推出。

DeepSeek V4的发布是需求激增的关键推动力。今年4月发布的DeepSeek V4大语言模型专门针对华为的Ascend架构及其CANN软件框架进行了优化，而非英伟达的CUDA生态系统。据《南华早报》报道，华为工程师在模型发布前直接与DeepSeek合作，华为确认其全系Ascend SuperNode产品线在第一天就适配了V4推理。阿里云和腾讯云在发布后数小时内就部署了V4服务。

950PR目前是唯一支持FP8压缩数值格式的中国产AI处理器，这种格式允许每秒执行更多运算并降低每次查询成本。V4采用混合专家架构，总参数高达1万亿，但每次推理仅激活约370亿参数。这种设计更偏好推理高效的硬件，恰好发挥950PR的优势，弥补其在原始训练吞吐量上的不足。DeepSeek为华为提供了早期优化权限，但未向英伟达或AMD提供同等支持。虽然V4的开放权重以兼容CUDA的标准格式发布，但DeepSeek自身的基础设施运行在华为Ascend芯片上。这一合作提前了中国云行业的采购时间线，950PR芯片价格因此上涨了约20%。

华为能否满足订单取决于中国领先代工厂中芯国际。中芯国际使用其N+3工艺（一种不使用EUV光刻的7nm级节点）制造950PR。华为今年目标生产约75万颗950PR，全面出货预计在2025年下半年，此前已于1月向客户交付样品，但这一数字预计仍将低于需求。中芯国际已持续一年多扩大先进节点产能，目标是在两年内实现五倍增长，将7nm和5nm月产能提升至10万片晶圆，到2030年达到50万片。此外，22nm及以下节点总产能可能从2025年的每月3万至5万片晶圆启动量增加至今年5万至6万片。华为正在建设两座专用晶圆厂，但所有权结构尚不明确。一旦全面投产，这些设施的产能可能超过中芯国际现有同类产线。

良品率仍是中国的痛点，中芯国际的7nm级工艺每片晶圆产出的合格芯片数量远低于台积电等效节点，且950PR的芯片尺寸可能比台积电等效产品大得多。中芯国际从晶圆开始到封装为Ascend处理器的周期时间也是一个问题，摩根大通估计目前约为八个月，而台积电类似节点只需三个月。此外，华为去年9月宣布与长鑫存储合作开发了自有HBM芯片，带宽高达1.6 TB/s（HiBL 1.0和HiZQ 2.0），但长鑫存储能多快提升竞争性HBM的产量仍是未知数。

黄仁勋在"特别竞争研究项目"的播客采访中承认："在中国，我们现在已经降到了零。"他批评美国出口政策"在很大程度上已经适得其反"，认为放弃中国这样规模的市场在战略上不明智。此前获美国政府许可向中国销售的H200芯片，在接到订单后未交付任何一颗。华盛顿和北京相互矛盾的监管要求导致海关陷入僵局：美国监管要求中国客户订购的H200芯片仅限在中国境内使用，而北京则指示国内科技公司将英伟达硬件限制在海外运营中使用。英伟达在FY2026年10-K文件中确认，其"实际上被排除在中国数据中心计算市场竞争之外"，并在当前展望中未假设来自该地区的任何数据中心计算收入。伯恩斯坦分析师估计，英伟达在中国AI GPU市场的份额可能从2024年的66%降至未来几年的约8%，这既源于美国限制，也因为国内供应商被要求覆盖高达80%的本土需求。TrendForce去年12月预测，2026年中国高端AI芯片市场将增长超过60%，国内供应商将占据约一半份额。

性能方面，950PR介于英伟达H100和H200之间，估计比受限的H20快2.8倍，但在计算和内存带宽上落后于H200。不过这一数据无法验证，因为Hopper时代硬件不支持原生FP4。华为通过光互连将大量处理器连接来弥补差距。其CloudMatrix 384系统将12个机架的Ascend模块组合成384处理器结构，提供约300 PFLOPS，但功耗是英伟达同等GB200配置的近四倍。950PR主要是推理芯片，而面向训练的950DT预计于第四季度推出，专为深度学习工作负载设计，可能缩小与英伟达Hopper代在模型训练任务上的差距。在该芯片出货前，需要在国内训练大型基础模型的中国企业面临推理芯片无法完全解决的约束。华为的CANN软件生态系统目前估计拥有超过400万开发者，但仍远小于英伟达CUDA的安装基数。CANN能否吸引足够的第三方开发以实现自我持续尚待观察。就目前而言，在中国市场，商业动力正朝着有利于华为的方向发展，而这仅仅是因为没有其他选择。

history.title: JSR to build first Taiwan photoresist plant to co-develop advanced resists with TSMC — multi-million dollar plant could come online as early as 2028
history.description: The facility will close a gap that has put JSR at a disadvantage relative to its two largest Japanese rivals.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter JSR, the Japanese chemical maker that controls roughly a fifth of the global photoresist market, plans to build its first production facility in Taiwan to supply and co-develop advanced photoresists with TSMC, Nikkei Asia reports. The company established a joint venture with a local partner in early April and aims to bring the plant online as early as 2028, investing tens of millions of dollars in the project.The facility will close a gap that has put JSR at a disadvantage relative to its two largest Japanese rivals. Tokyo Ohka Kogyo (TOK) and Shin-Etsu Chemical both already operate production facilities in Taiwan, where they work directly with TSMC on resist development.Photoresist is the light-sensitive material used to transfer circuit patterns onto silicon wafers during lithography. At advanced process nodes, the resist must be precisely tuned to work with specific exposure tools and etch chemistries, and that tuning requires constant back-and-forth between the resist supplier and the foundry.Article continues below You may like Rapidus secures $1.7 billion from Japan’s government and private investors for 2nm chip production Imec's new post-exposure bake method speeds up EUV chipmaking tools, boosting production for the most advanced chips Taiwan rejects possibility of transferring 40% of the island's semiconductor capacity to U.S. JSR currently develops products for Taiwanese customers by shipping samples from its facilities in Japan, the U.S., and Belgium. Each round trip can take weeks, according to Nikkei, dragging out an iteration process that its competitors can complete far more quickly from local production sites. A Taiwan plant would let JSR embed its engineers closer to TSMC's development teams, a model TOK and Shin-Etsu have already adopted. Beyond photoresist, JSR told Nikkei Asia that it’s also considering producing other materials at the Taiwan site, such as abrasives used to smooth semiconductor substrates.JSR's Taiwan expansion is part of a broader expansion with the company simultaneously building the world’s first production-scale facility for metal oxide resist (MOR) in South Korea, with mass production expected to begin this year. That plant will supply Samsung Electronics and SK hynix with tin-based MOR for EUV lithography.MOR absorbs EUV photons more efficiently than the conventional chemically amplified resists used at older nodes, enabling higher resolution with fewer pattern defects. JSR acquired MOR pioneer Inpria in 2021 and has been developing tin-oxide-based formulations since. The company plans to market MOR to TSMC as well, according to Nikkei, putting itself in place for the next generation of EUV and high-NA EUV production lines that TSMC will need at 2nm and beyond.Japanese companies collectively hold around 80% of the global photoresist market and dominate the high-end EUV segment almost entirely. Chinese firms have made inroads at the KrF and i-line level, but their penetration at ArF and above remains minimal.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors"Chinese players are a threat, but it'll still be some time before they can catch up with us and take market share," Toru Kimura, a senior officer at JSR who heads the company's electronic materials business, told Nikkei. JSR's strategy seems to be to stay ahead by locking in co-development relationships at the leading edge, which have the highest technical barriers to entry. history.author: Luke James
history.titleZh: JSR将在台湾建造首座光刻胶工厂，与台积电共同开发先进光刻胶——这座价值数百万美元的工厂最早可能于2028年投产
history.descriptionZh:
日本化学材料巨头JSR（信越化学工业株式会社旗下）计划在台湾建设其首座光刻胶生产基地，以强化与台积电（TSMC）在先进制程领域的合作。据《日经亚洲》报道，JSR已于4月初与当地合作伙伴成立合资公司，预计投资数千万美元，目标在2028年前让工厂投产。此举旨在弥补JSR相对于两大日本竞争对手——东京应化工业（TOK）和信越化学——的劣势，这两家公司早已在台湾设立工厂，并与台积电直接开展光刻胶联合开发。

光刻胶是光刻工艺中将电路图案转移到硅片上的关键感光材料。在先进制程节点（如3nm及以下），光刻胶必须针对特定的曝光设备和蚀刻化学配方进行精密调谐，这需要光刻胶供应商与代工厂之间进行高频次的反复迭代。目前，JSR从日本、美国、比利时的基地向台湾客户发送样品，单次往返运输可能耗时数周，严重拖慢了迭代速度；而竞争对手从本地工厂出发，能更快完成优化。台湾新厂将使JSR的工程师更贴近台积电的研发团队，实现类似TOK和信越化学的“嵌入式”合作模式。除光刻胶外，JSR还考虑在台湾生产用于半导体衬底平坦化的研磨剂等材料。

JSR的台湾扩张是其全球布局的一部分：该公司同时在韩国建设世界首座量产级金属氧化物光刻胶（MOR）工厂，预计今年开始量产，将向三星电子和SK海力士供应基于锡的MOR，用于极紫外（EUV）光刻。MOR相比传统化学放大光刻胶（CAR），能更高效吸收EUV光子，在更高分辨率下减少图形缺陷。JSR于2021年收购了MOR先驱Inpria，并持续开发氧化锡配方。据《日经亚洲》透露，JSR也计划向台积电推广MOR，为2nm及以下节点所需的下一代EUV和高数值孔径（High-NA）EUV产线做准备。

日本公司整体占据全球光刻胶市场约80%的份额，在高端EUV领域几乎完全主导。中国企业在KrF和i-line光刻胶方面有所突破，但在ArF及以上级别的渗透仍微乎其微。JSR高级官员、电子材料业务负责人木村透表示：“中国玩家是威胁，但他们要追赶上我们并夺取市场份额，还需要相当长时间。”JSR的策略是通过在技术壁垒最高的前沿节点锁定联合开发关系来保持领先。这一布局不仅关乎供应链效率，更是在下一代光刻材料竞争中巩固日本阵营的技术护城河。

history.title: Apple considering Intel and Samsung for US chip production, report claims — consumer electronics giant looks to diversify supply chain amid chip shortages
history.description: Apple is reportedly in early talks with Intel and Samsung to secure more production for its advanced chips, as the company is constrained by the limited availability of advanced nodes that its SoCs are produced on.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Apple is reportedly talking with Intel and Samsung to produce processors for the company as it’s grappling with a shortage of chips for its latest products. According to Bloomberg, Cupertino has had multiple early-stage discussions with Team Blue, while some key Apple executives have also visited a Samsung plant that’s still in development — likely its Taylor, Texas, fab, which is set to start risk production this year.None of the talks have resulted in any orders, so far, the report states. There have been concerns about using non-TSMC tech in Apple products, especially as it has been producing chips for the iPhone since the A8 used in the iPhone 6 and iPhone 6 Plus. However, the AI infrastructure build-out is negatively affecting the company, with Apple CEO Tim Cook conceding that it’s constrained by TSMC’s supply of advanced chips. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said that his company has dethroned the consumer electronics giant as TSMC’s number one customer, and even alluded that Apple might have to pay more for its chips. But even before this, Cupertino has reportedly been considering using Intel’s 18A process to build future M-series chips, especially as global geopolitical events are threatening the stability of the global supply chain.If we look at Apple’s history, both Samsung and Intel have closely worked with the company for years. The first iPhone, all the way to the iPhone 5S, was powered by Samsung chips, while Macs and MacBooks used x86 Intel processors from 2006 until they were replaced by Apple silicon in 2023. If successful, these talks would resume Apple’s partnership with one (or both) of the companies.Article continues below You may like US gov't warned Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang, Tim Cook, and Lisa Su that China could invade Taiwan by 2027 Apple warns Mac mini and Mac Studio shortages could last for months Leading PC manufacturers considering using Chinese memory chips, report claims This would be a win for Intel as it’s looking to land a major customer for its foundry business — something that it fumbled in 2011 when Cupertino first approached it for its custom chip needs. A move like this could also help bolster Samsung’s foundry business, as it sits at a distant second place when compared to TSMC. At the same time, it would reduce Apple’s risk of relying on a single supplier for the majority of its advanced chip supply. Even though TSMC’s Arizona plant is ramping up production and it’s estimated that it will deliver 100 million chips for Cupertino this year, it is just a fraction of Apple’s demands, with the majority of its advanced SoCs still expected to come from Taiwan.Tim Cook has been well aware of the situation with TSMC for years now. He even said in a 2022 all-hands company meeting, “Regardless of what you may feel or think, 60% coming out of anywhere is probably not a strategic position.” This was likely highlighted with the AI-driven chip shortage. “The primary constraint is the availability of the advanced nodes our SoCs are produced on, not memory,” the Apple CEO said during the company’s latest earnings call. “I believe it will take several months to reach supply-demand balance.”But even if Apple were to strike a deal with both Intel and Samsung today, it will take some time for production to ramp up, and consumers won’t feel its effect for several months, if not a couple of years. history.author: Jowi Morales
history.titleZh: 报道称苹果考虑让英特尔和三星在美国生产芯片——消费电子巨头希望在芯片短缺之际实现供应链多元化
history.descriptionZh:
苹果公司正与英特尔和三星就处理器生产事宜进行洽谈，以应对其最新产品面临的芯片供应短缺问题。据彭博社报道，这家库比蒂诺巨头已与蓝色巨人（英特尔）进行了多次早期阶段的讨论，部分苹果高管还访问了尚处于建设中的三星工厂，很可能是其位于德克萨斯州泰勒的晶圆厂，该厂计划于今年开始风险试产。截至目前，这些谈判尚未产生任何订单。外界对苹果产品采用非台积电技术一直存在担忧，尤其是自iPhone 6和iPhone 6 Plus搭载的A8芯片以来，台积电一直为iPhone生产芯片。然而，人工智能基础设施的扩展正在对苹果产生负面影响，苹果CEO蒂姆·库克坦言公司受到台积电先进芯片供应紧张的制约。英伟达CEO黄仁勋表示，他的公司已取代这家消费电子巨头成为台积电的头号客户，甚至暗示苹果可能需要为其芯片支付更高价格。但在此之前，苹果据报道已在考虑使用英特尔的18A工艺来制造未来的M系列芯片，尤其是考虑到全球地缘政治事件正威胁全球供应链的稳定性。

回顾苹果的历史，三星和英特尔都与其有过多年紧密合作。从初代iPhone到iPhone 5S，均采用三星芯片；而Mac和MacBook从2006年至2023年替换为苹果自研芯片期间，一直使用英特尔的x86处理器。如果谈判成功，将恢复苹果与其中一家或两家公司的合作关系。

这对英特尔来说将是一场胜利，因为它正寻求为其代工业务赢得一个重大客户——而2011年苹果首次接触英特尔定制芯片需求时，英特尔错失了这一机会。此举也有助于提振三星的代工业务，因为与台积电相比，三星目前仅位居第二，且差距明显。同时，这将降低苹果在先进芯片供应上过度依赖单一供应商的风险。尽管台积电位于亚利桑那州的工厂正在加速生产，预计今年将为苹果交付1亿颗芯片，但这仅占苹果需求的一小部分，其大部分先进SoC仍将来自台湾。

蒂姆·库克多年来一直清楚台积电的处境。他在2022年的一次全体公司会议上甚至表示：“无论你怎么感受或思考，60%的供应来自任何一个地方可能都不是一个战略性的位置。”这一点在AI驱动的芯片短缺中尤为凸显。库克在公司最近一次财报电话会议上表示：“主要制约因素是我们的SoC所依赖的先进制程节点的可用性，而不是存储器。我相信需要几个月时间才能实现供需平衡。”

但即使苹果今天与英特尔和三星达成协议，产能爬坡仍需要时间，消费者在几个月甚至一两年内都无法感受到其影响。

history.title: Inland QN450 1TB SSD Review: Maximum efficiency, minimum spend
history.description: The Inland QN450 is a surprisingly capable Gen 4 drive with strong all-around performance and exceptional power efficiency. It outshines the NV3 but has hardware uncertainty and low TBW.
history.content: Inland remains a reliable partner in this volatile SSD environment, offering decent drives with good warranties at competitive prices. The QN450, a drive by its designation, which should be relegated to secondary storage, surprises. Going by its specifications, it looks like yet another cheap SSD to lure in budget shoppers, but, in fact, under this misleading varnish is an excellent drive that would otherwise escape notice. This is one to keep on your buy lists.The surprises are quick and meaningful. Performance across the board, even with sustained writes, is very good. The drive also delivers very high power efficiency, which is crucial for a drive that will end up in laptops or the PS5. It’s also affordable, which makes sense given its market positioning – it is meant to be a Kingston NV3 killer, the drive you reach for when you just need something to complete your build – but it frankly performs closer to the high side of the stack. For the price, that’s hard to beat.The downsides? Given the specifications and positioning, we would have to expect variable hardware builds over the life of the product, which means the underlying components can change. You might get what we did, or you might get something worse. We highly recommend checking your drive when you get it. This unfortunately requires some research on your part, but that’s standard for the industry at this point. More directly, the QN450 has a relatively low write endurance rating (TBW) for its class. This could suggest that it is meant to use QLC flash even though our sample had high-performance TLC. Some caution is therefore warranted, although we would say that, given the pricing at the time of review, this drive would compete favorably against QLC-based drives in its segment.Inland QN450 SpecificationsSwipe to scroll horizontallyProduct500GB1TB2TB4TBPricing$124.99$194.99$314.99$449.99Form FactorM.2 2280 (Single-sided)M.2 2280 (Single-sided)M.2 2280 (Single-sided)M.2 2280 (Single-sided)Interface / ProtocolPCIe 4.0 x4NVMe 2.0PCIe 4.0 x4NVMe 2.0PCIe 4.0 x4NVMe 2.0PCIe 4.0 x4NVMe 2.0ControllerN/ASilicon Motion SM2268XT2Realtek RTS5772DL (listed)Phison E21T (listed)DRAMN/A (HMB)N/A (HMB)N/A (HMB)N/A (HMB)Flash MemoryN/AMicron 232-Layer TLC (B58R)Micron 232-Layer QLC (N58R)Micron 232-Layer QLC (N58R)Sequential ReadN/A5,000 MB/s5,000 MB/s5,000 MB/sSequential WriteN/A4,000 MB/s4,000 MB/s4,200 MB/sRandom ReadN/A300K IOPS300K IOPS600K IOPSRandom WriteN/A450K IOPS450K IOPS800K IOPSActive Power (Avg)N/A5.3W5.3W4WEnduranceN/A300TBW600TBW900TBWPart Number618996784523618996774760618996774777618996757039Warranty6-Year6-Year6-Year6-YearToday's best Inland QN450 1TB SDD deals $194.99ViewINLAND QN450 SSD 1000GB M.2 2280 NVMe...INLAND QN450 SSD 1000GB M.2 2280 NVMe SSD- PCIe 4.0 Gen4x4, Up to 5,000 MB/s - Internal Solid State DriveWe check over 250 million products every day for the best pricesThe Inland QN450 is available in four capacities: 500GB, 1TB, 2TB, and 4TB. With modern drives, you usually want a larger drive for the best performance, but it doesn’t seem too critical here. 1TB and up is good enough. The drive is priced at $94.99, $169.99, $289.99, and $449.99. Are these prices good? Hmm, they’re okay. The Biwin Black Opal NV7400 is often the better deal, and the Biwin NV7200 can also be a good choice. At least at 1TB and 2TB. At 4TB, the QN450 is more compelling with the current price, as it basically beats everything.The drive’s not fast, though. At least not on paper. It reaches a maximum of 5,000 / 4,200 MB/s for sequential reads and writes and 600K / 800K random read and write IOPS. Only at 4TB, though. The performance specifications at lower capacities are worse. Then again, we’re not sure how seriously you can take those numbers. Inland reports the drive as having the Realtek RTS5772 – which isn’t great – and the Phison E21T for controller options. However, our sample has the Silicon Motion SM2268XT2. This controller is extremely capable, and the flash drive it uses is also pretty good. This makes the performance specs seem kind of conservative, to say the least.Inland stands out with a six-year warranty, one year more than the usual, but only 300TB of writes per TB capacity. This is plenty for a budget, QLC-based SSD, but our sample has TLC flash. This makes us wonder if this is an “NV3 effect” where we can’t be 100% sure what hardware the drive will deliver. Either way, this level of write endurance is very weak for TLC flash. However, our analysis of the hardware indicates that the quality is not of a lower grade at all. We have to believe the TBW is this low to accommodate the potential use of QLC flash, which is completely what we expected from a “QN” drive in the first place.Inland QN450 Software and AccessoriesOne big downside of Inland SSDs is the total lack of software support. We recommend MultiDrive if you’re looking for a modern data backup system, but Clonezilla is more widely supported and is bootable. We recommend CrystalDiskInfo for general drive health checking.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsInland QN450: A Closer LookImage 1 of 2(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)A simple, understated, single-sided drive. The lettering on the PCB indicates this is using the Silicon Motion SM2268XT2 controller with BGA308 form factor flash – times two – in the M.2 2280 form factor. No frills, which we actually like.Image 1 of 3(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Controller confirmed, two NAND flash packages confirmed. SMI’s SM2268XT2 is a four-channel, DRAM-less SSD controller that can compete with the likes of Phison’s E27T and Maxio’s MAP1602. Those two are excellent controllers that can get hit over 7 GB/s with the right flash. The SM2268XT2 is no different, but it’s often seen leaning more budget than its peers. The Biwin M350 is a good example, as is the Kingston NV3. We found in our M350 review that the hardware, as specified, is selling itself short – it can bring some decent performance numbers.We expect the QN450 to be no different, as 5 GB/s with this controller and flash is simply significantly below what the hardware can do. Often, hardware will be undersold like this so that you can have hardware swaps down the line – that is, different controllers and flash that can meet a basic spec. That might very well be the case here, as Inland lists the drive as using the Realtek RTS5772DL – a passable but not very good controller – and the Phison E21T, which is good but older. In fact, the main reason to use the SM2268XT2 would be to pair it with faster flash like the 232-Layer Micron TLC that we have here. So, yes, hardware swapping remains open given what Inland lists, but a manufacturer could also run the drive slower with better hardware for other reasons, such as extending the lifespan of weaker flash. However, we discovered nothing to suggest that is the case here; in fact, the configuration of this flash is quite performant with good endurance.MORE: Best SSDsMORE: Best External SSDsMORE: Best SSD for the Steam Deck
history.author: Shane Downing
history.titleZh: 内陆 QN450 1TB 固态硬盘评测：最高效率，最低花费
history.descriptionZh:
在波动不定的SSD市场中，Inland依然是一个可靠的合作伙伴，以有竞争力的价格提供质量可靠、保修期长的硬盘。其QN450型号最初被定位为仅适合用作辅助存储，但实际表现却令人惊喜。从规格上看，它似乎又是一款吸引预算有限消费者的廉价SSD，然而在这种误导性外表之下，隐藏着一款性能出色、容易被忽视的硬盘——它值得列入你的购买清单。

惊喜来得直接而有力：整体性能非常出色，即使在持续写入场景下也表现良好。该硬盘还拥有极高的能效，这对于最终用于笔记本电脑或PlayStation 5的场景至关重要。价格也很实惠，符合其市场定位——它旨在成为Kingston NV3的“杀手”，是你在组装电脑时所需的那块基础硬盘——但坦率地说，它的性能表现更接近高端梯队。以这个价位来看，很难被超越。

缺点呢？考虑到其规格和定位，我们必须预料到在产品生命周期内硬件配置可能会变化，这意味着底层组件有可能更换。你拿到的硬盘可能与我们的测试样品一致，也可能更差。我们强烈建议在购买后检查自己的硬盘。这不幸地需要你做一些研究，但这是当前行业的常态。更直接的问题是，QN450在该级别中拥有相对较低的写入耐久度（TBW）。这可能表明它预期使用QLC闪存，尽管我们的样品使用的是高性能TLC。因此需要保持一定谨慎，但考虑到评测时的定价，该硬盘在其细分市场中完全可以与基于QLC的产品竞争。

规格方面：QN450提供500GB、1TB、2TB和4TB四种容量，定价分别为124.99、194.99、314.99和449.99美元（文中另有不同价格数据，以评测时为准）。接口为PCIe 4.0 x4 / NVMe 2.0，M.2 2280单面规格。控制器方面，1TB样品采用Silicon Motion SM2268XT2，2TB和4TB则标注为Realtek RTS5772DL和Phison E21T。所有型号均无DRAM（采用HMB技术）。闪存方面，1TB样品为Micron 232层TLC（B58R），2TB和4TB为Micron 232层QLC（N58R）。顺序读取最高5,000 MB/s，顺序写入1TB为4,000 MB/s，4TB为4,200 MB/s。随机读写IOPS最高分别达600K和800K（4TB）。平均活动功耗约5.3W。写入耐久度：1TB为300TBW，2TB为600TBW，4TB为900TBW。保修期为6年，比常见多一年。

性能表现上，理论值并不突出（5,000 / 4,200 MB/s），但实际测试中，由于采用了Silicon Motion SM2268XT2控制器和高质量的Micron TLC闪存，实际性能远超这一保守规格。这款控制器本身性能强劲（四通道、无DRAM），可与Phison E27T、Maxio MAP1602等优秀控制器媲美，理论上配合优质闪存可达到7GB/s以上。Inland选择将其性能限制在5GB/s，很可能为了在后期进行硬件替换（例如更换为Realtek或Phison的较低端控制器）时仍能满足标称规格。这种策略降低了供应链风险，但用户的实际体验可能因批次不同而有所差异。

技术影响和应用场景：QN450最大的亮点是在低价位实现了接近高端SSD的性能和能效，非常适合用于笔记本电脑、游戏主机（如PS5）或预算型台式机。其极高的能效比意味着在移动设备中能延长电池续航。不足之处在于：（1）缺乏官方软件支持（建议使用MultiDrive、Clonezilla或CrystalDiskInfo等其他工具）；（2）写入耐久度较低（300TBW/1TB对于TLC闪存而言偏低），这可能是为了兼容QLC闪存而设定的安全阈值，但对于普通家用或游戏用途已足够；（3）硬件不确定性依然存在，用户需自行验证实际配置。

总体而言，Inland QN450是一款“真香”硬盘——实际上它被严重低估了。如果购买的批次恰好使用了高性能的SM2268XT2控制器和TLC闪存，那么它将是同类产品中的性价比之王，甚至能与更高价位的竞品一较高下。但用户需要做好功课，确保自己拿到的是理想配置。

history.title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang says China should not have Blackwell or Rubin AI GPUs — firmly states US should have 'the first, the most, and the best' when it comes to AI hardware
history.description: Nvidia's Jensen Huang reiterates that American companies should be able to ship their products globally, but maintains that the latest AI accelerators should remain in America.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 12 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang has firmly stated that China should not have access to its "latest and greatest" AI GPU technology, notably its Blackwell and Rubin lines, arguing that the United States should have a lead in AI technology. As per Nikkei Asia, Huang says Nvidia are "huge supporters of the United States having the first, the most, and the best."The comments by the head of Nvidia came during the Milken Institute Global Conference in Los Angeles, an event attended by bankers, businessmen, and investors. He stated that the global reach of American AI accelerators helps increase tax income, which strengthens the economy and in turn supports national security. At the same time, Huang emphasized that China should not receive the 'latest and greatest' AI accelerators, such as the current-generation Blackwell or next-generation Rubin. This is a way for the U.S. to maintain its lead in the frontier AI models."the United States [should have] the first, the most, and the best"Jensen HuangBack in November, the U.S. government approved sales of Nvidia's H200 processors to approved customers in China, provided that they would pay 25% cut to the U.S. government. The company confirmed in February that it had received orders from numerous customers in the People's Republic. But in its most recent conference call, Nvidia said it had not shipped any H200 processors to Chinese customers recently. By contrast, AMD, which also got permission to sell its Instinct MI308X, sold $390 million worth of AI GPUs to its clients in China.Article continues below You may like Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang ‘nearly lost his composure’ when pressed on selling chips to China — ‘You’re not talking to someone who woke up a loser’ Jensen says Nvidia now has 'zero percent' market share in China — says US export policy 'has already largely backfired' US senators want to suspend Nvidia AI chip export licenses to China and its intermediaries More recently, Huang admitted that Nvidia's AI accelerator market share in China had dropped to zero percent, suggesting that the company did not ship any H200 GPUs to the PRC in the first calendar quarter of 2026.There might be a reason for Nvidia to avoid shipping Hopper GPUs to China: Both Hopper and Blackwell processors are made at the same fabs and N4/N5-capable production lines by TSMC, and since capacity is limited, Nvidia aims to sell more advanced and expensive Blackwell processors to its customers in the U.S. This in terms means avoiding the U.S. government's 25% cut rather than selling Hopper GPUs to clients in China.Once Nvidia ramps up production of its next-generation Rubin GPUs using TSMC's N3 process technology, the Taiwan-based foundry will be able to allocate more N4/N5 capacity to Hopper GPUs. It's possible to speculate that Nvidia will likely resume shipments of Hopper accelerators to China.In any case, earlier this year, Jensen Huang said that Nvidia's clients in China would get Blackwell GPUs in time, but did not elaborate, which probably means that when Rubin GPUs are available widely, some cut-down versions of Blackwell may be supplied to customers in the PRC.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Anton Shilov
history.titleZh: 英伟达CEO黄仁勋表示中国不应拥有Blackwell或Rubin AI GPU——坚称美国在AI硬件方面应“最先、最多、最好”
history.descriptionZh:
在近期于洛杉矶举行的米尔肯研究所全球会议上，英伟达首席执行官黄仁勋明确表示，中国不应获得美国“最新、最好”的AI GPU技术，特别是当前世代的Blackwell和下一代的Rubin系列，他认为美国应在人工智能技术上保持领先。黄仁勋强调：“美国应该拥有第一、最多和最好的。”这一表态反映了英伟达对美国出口管制政策的支持立场。

### 背景与上下文
美国政府在2024年11月批准了英伟达向中国特定客户销售H200处理器，但要求客户向美国政府缴纳25%的税款。英伟达在2月确认收到中国多家客户的订单，但在最近一次财报电话会议上表示，近期并未向中国客户发货任何H200处理器。相比之下，同样获得销售许可的AMD，已向中国客户销售了价值3.9亿美元的Instinct MI308X AI GPU。黄仁勋随后承认，英伟达在中国的AI加速器市场份额已降至零，暗示在2026年第一季度并未向中国出货任何H200 GPU。

### 核心技术原理与创新点
文中涉及的AI加速器芯片包括：
- **H200（Hopper架构）**：基于台积电N4/N5工艺，专为大模型训练和推理设计，支持高带宽内存（HBM3e），是当前被允许对华销售的“降级”产品，但仍属高性能计算芯片。
- **Blackwell（当前旗舰）**：英伟达最新一代AI GPU，采用更先进的架构，大幅提升算力和能效，是目前美国对华出口管制的核心对象。
- **Rubin（下一代）**：计划采用台积电N3工艺，代表未来AI计算最高水平，同样被列入限制清单。

黄仁勋认为，阻止中国获得这些顶级芯片是维持美国在前沿AI模型领域领导地位的关键手段。

### 性能参数与对比数据
- **制程差异**：H200与Blackwell均使用台积电N4/N5工艺线，产能共享；Rubin将转向更先进的N3工艺，届时N4/N5产能将释放，可重新用于生产H200。
- **市场表现**：英伟达因出口管制主动放弃中国市场，导致其在中国市占率从高位骤降至零；AMD则通过合规销售获得3.9亿美元营收，表明中国市场需求旺盛。
- **税率影响**：若英伟达向中国销售H200，需缴纳25%给美国政府，而直接销售Blackwell给美国客户则无此加价，因此英伟达更优先满足美国市场需求。

### 技术影响与应用场景
- **美国视角**：黄仁勋支持出口管制，认为美国AI芯片的全球销售可增加税收，强化经济与国家安全。限制中国获得最先进芯片可确保美国在军事、科研、商业等领域的AI模型创新领先。
- **中国市场困境**：尽管英伟达曾表示中国客户“终将获得Blackwell GPU”，但未给出时间表。分析认为，预计在Rubin大规模量产后，台积电N4/N5产能富余，英伟达才可能恢复向中国供应H200或推出阉割版Blackwell。
- **产业链影响**：英伟达的策略导致中国AI企业不得不转向国产芯片或AMD替代品，加速了中国自主GPU生态发展，但短期内面临性能差距。同时，英伟达因牺牲中国市场份额而承受机会成本，需权衡政治合规与商业利益。

### 总结与展望
黄仁勋的言论反映了美国政商界在AI竞争上的共识：通过出口管制维持技术代差。然而，英伟达不向中国出货并非完全出于政治原因——更现实的考量是台积电产能有限，优先将高利润的Blackwell留给美国客户，避免25%的政府抽成。随着Rubin投产，N4/N5产能释放后，英伟达很可能重新向中国供应H200，甚至提供功能削减版的Blackwell。这一动态将持续塑造全球AI芯片市场的竞争格局。

history.title: China pushes for 70% homegrown silicon wafer use as domestic firm ramps up 12-inch production — government seeking to localize critical chip supply chain amid AI boom and export restrictions
history.description: China is targeting 70% local wafer sourcing as firms like Eswin scale 12-inch production, aiming to reduce reliance on foreign suppliers and support growing AI chip demand.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter A report by Nikkei Asia claims that China wants 70 percent of the silicon wafers used by its chipmakers this year to be sourced from domestic suppliers. The move marks another step in the country’s increasingly urgent push to localize its semiconductor supply chain, as chips become ever more critical in the age of AI.According to the report, reliable sources say the Chinese government's target has become an unspoken mandate among chipmakers to use locally made 12-inch wafers. "Only 30% of the market will still be open to foreign players. Some Chinese chipmakers are still aiming to produce more advanced chips, and that part of the market still requires foreign market leaders' support," a chip industry executive reportedly told Nikkei Asia. "But for the local market in mature and legacy chips, basically local Chinese silicon wafers can already meet the demand and requirements.”Earlier this month, we reported that Huawei was on track to challenge NVIDIA in the Chinese market, buoyed by rising demand and growing pressure from Beijing for tech firms to prioritize domestic suppliers. These developments are part of a broader, coordinated push to reduce reliance on foreign players across the semiconductor stack.Article continues below You may like Huawei could seize China’s AI chip crown in 2026 as Nvidia’s H200 shipments stall in regulatory limbo China to increase leading-edge chip output by 5x in two years Nvidia market share in China falls to less than 60% Today, many of the most critical segments remain dominated by overseas companies. Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix lead the global memory market, while Intel and NVIDIA remain dominant in high-performance computing chips. Even at the materials level, the supply chain is still largely controlled abroad, with the two largest silicon wafer manufacturers — Shin-Etsu Chemical and SUMCO — both based in Japan.Together, these companies command a significant share of the global semiconductor supply chain, underscoring the scale of China’s challenge as it seeks to build a fully localized ecosystem.China is already largely self-reliant in producing 8-inch silicon wafers, which are typically used for older-generation chips and certain power electronics. However, competing at the cutting edge requires access to more advanced 12-inch (300 mm) wafers, which are essential for manufacturing high-performance logic and memory chips. China still depends heavily on foreign suppliers for these larger wafers. We reported late last month that the US government is preparing to further block the export of advanced AI chips to China, further intensifying the country’s push to localize its semiconductor supply chain.According to the report, China’s push into advanced wafer manufacturing is being led by Xi'an Eswin Material Technology, which is targeting 1.2 million 12-inch wafers per month by 2026 — enough to meet about 40 percent of domestic demand and push its global share past 10 percent. The report highlights a wider expansion across players such as National Silicon Industry Group, Zhonghuan Advanced, and Hangzhou Lion Microelectronics, with Eswin described as the most aggressive, adding roughly 700,000 wafers per month through new facilities in Xi’an and Wuhan.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsThe report said Eswin already supplies several major domestic chipmakers, including Semiconductor Manufacturing International Corporation (SMIC), and is increasingly becoming the default supplier for new fabs. It also claims links to global customers such as Micron Technology and United Microelectronics Corporation, while Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix are validating its wafers.The report also added that Chinese foundries, alongside Huawei-linked firms, are ramping production of advanced chips — roughly in the 7 nm to 5 nm class — to meet AI demand. However, some production still depends on foreign wafers. It cites estimates from Bernstein Research suggesting that China met about 50 percent of its 12-inch wafer demand by 2025, with domestic players’ global capacity share rising from 3 percent in 2020 to 28 percent in 2025, and potentially to 32 percent by 2026. history.author: Etiido Uko
history.titleZh: 中国推动国产硅片使用率达70%，国内企业扩大12英寸生产——政府寻求在人工智能热潮和出口限制下实现关键芯片供应链本土化
history.descriptionZh:
据《日经亚洲》报道，中国正加速推进半导体供应链本土化，要求国内芯片制造商在今年实现70%的硅片（尤其是12英寸硅片）由本土供应商提供。这一目标已成为业界不成文的规定，仅剩30%的市场份额留给外国厂商。中国在8英寸硅片（主要用于成熟制程和电源管理芯片）领域已基本实现自给自足，但12英寸（300毫米）硅片作为制造高性能逻辑和存储芯片的关键材料，仍高度依赖日本信越化学、SUMCO等海外供应商。此举是美国持续收紧对华先进AI芯片出口限制背景下，中国构建自主半导体生态的又一关键步骤。

核心技术方面，12英寸硅片是先进制程（如7nm至5nm）芯片的基础衬底材料，其晶格纯度、平整度及缺陷密度直接影响芯片良率和性能。中国本土企业正通过大规模扩产突破技术壁垒：西安奕斯伟材料科技（Eswin）被描述为“最激进”的玩家，计划到2026年实现月产120万片12英寸硅片，足以满足国内约40%的需求，并将全球市场份额提升至10%以上（当前从2020年的3%跃升至2025年的28%，有望2026年达32%）。其扩产主要依托西安和武汉的新工厂，月产能增加约70万片。其他主要参与者包括国家硅产业集团、中环领先和杭州立昂微电子。性能与市场数据方面，伯恩斯坦研究公司估算，2025年中国12英寸硅片自给率约为50%；而Eswin已向中芯国际（SMIC）等本土代工厂稳定供货，并成为新晶圆厂的默认供应商。其产品还获得美光、联电等国际客户的认可，三星和SK海力士正在进行验证。但值得注意的是，中国在先进制程（7nm-5nm）领域仍依赖海外硅片，尤其是类似华为关联企业正加速生产AI芯片，但部分生产仍需外国供应商支持。

技术影响与应用场景上，这一动向标志着中国正从成熟制程（90nm-28nm）向先进制程全面渗透。在AI加速、高性能计算、汽车电子等场景，12英寸硅片是实现高能效比和集成度的核心基础。中国通过提升本土硅片产能，可降低对日韩供应链的依赖，同时支撑如华为昇腾等AI芯片的产量扩充——后者正试图在2026年挑战英伟达在中国的市场地位。然而，报告也指出，在高端逻辑芯片和存储芯片领域，英特尔、英伟达、三星、SK海力士等海外巨头仍占据主导，中国本土硅片当前更多服务于成熟和传统芯片市场，先进制程环节仍需海外领导者支持。整体来看，中国半导体自主化进程正从材料端（硅片）切入，逐步构建从设备到设计的完整闭环，而12英寸硅片的国产化突破将成为决定这一战略成败的关键节点。

history.title: Enthusiast recreates 43-year-old Apple Lisa with FPGA board — first commercial computer with a GUI faithfully cloned with modernized machine
history.description: An enthusiast has taken the time to recreate the Apple Lisa, the first commercial PC with a GUI, with an FPGA board.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 3 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Many think that Apple's history creating computers with graphical interfaces starts with the venerable Macintosh, but the company's first foray into designing GUIs actually started with 1983's Lisa. The machine was the first to bring a popular window application system and mouse control to general availability at an edible (if bitter) price of $9,995 dollars of 1983 vintage, equivalent to nearly $34,000 today.Thanks to enthusiast and Youtuber Alex Anderson-McLeod, you'll soon be able to roll your own much-improved LisaFPGA clone. Alex designed a roomy one-board system centered around an Artix 7-100T FPGA as the Lisa's brains, coupled to 2MB of SRAM and emulated hard drive, floppy, serial, and naturally, keyboard and mouse connectors. I recreated the Apple Lisa computer inside an FPGA! - The LisaFPGA Project - YouTube Watch On The clone is far superior to the original, thanks to its reliance on modern hardware and Alex's design smarts. For starters, you don't need a video converter, as the board natively outputs video HDMI with a scanline option; both its main video modes are switchable on-the-fly. Although the board includes original-spec connectors for input devices, it also helpfully has USB ports for keyboard and mouse so you don't have to procure original versions.Article continues below You may like Computer History Museum unveils comically large-scale rendition of the 1986 Apple Macintosh Plus Retro Apple Mac mod implements thermal printer floppy swap Tiny Mac look-a-like alarm clock transformed to run real Mac software In a similar fashion, the serial ports can be redirected to the main USB-C connector thanks to the inclusion of a USB hub, saving you having to find USB-to-serial adapters and chunky DB25 connectors. Floppy images can be loaded from an SD card, direct connection to PC, or an original floppy drive connected to the the corresponding port.Even for its time, the Lisa was pretty slow, thanks to its software design choices and and use of the 5 MHz variant of the Motorola 68000 CPU. As a nod to these limitations, Alex included two overclocking multipliers that can take the machine to the equivalent of 75 MHz, likewise selectable on the fly with physical switches.The revision of the LisaFPGA featured in the video is version 2, but Alex says that version 3 is coming soon with a handful of fixes. He says the project will be fully open-sourced on Github as soon as he gets his proverbial ducks in a row. He's also considering selling the clones, and will be speaking about them at Vintage Computer Festival Southwest later in the month.Although it was generally considered a failure due to its price and software availability, the Lisa paved the way for 1984's Macintosh, the $2,459 machine that popularized graphical interfaces and the Apple brand worldwide. The Lisa computer had an undignified ending, too, with 2,700 of them dumped in Logan, Utah, as part of a tax write-off on unsold inventory. Still, similar to how the first iPhone had major issues in hindsight, the Lisa broke ground for much greater things.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsAlthough Apple popularized graphical home computers for the masses, Apple did not invent the GUI, nor the mouse. The GUI was Xerox's doing at its PARC laboratories, that Steve Jobs visited and, cough, cribbed notes from. The Xerox Star 8010 workstation was the first computer with both technologies, but it was sold as part of a larger package and commanded the steep price of $16,595 in 1981, or around $60,000 today. As for the mouse, it was designed at the Stanford Research institute much earlier in 1963.
history.author: Bruno Ferreira
history.titleZh: 爱好者用FPGA板重现43年前的Apple Lisa——首款配备图形用户界面的商用计算机被忠实克隆并现代化改造
history.descriptionZh:
1983年，苹果公司推出了Lisa电脑，这是世界上首款将图形用户界面（GUI）和鼠标控制普及到大众市场的个人电脑。然而，其高达9,995美元（相当于今天的近34,000美元）的售价和有限的软件生态，使它最终被视为商业失败，约2,700台库存甚至被当作税务注销品掩埋。尽管如此，Lisa为1984年大获成功的Macintosh铺平了道路，后者以2,459美元的价格将GUI真正带入千家万户。如今，一位名叫Alex Anderson-McLeod的爱好者兼YouTube博主，利用现代FPGA技术完整复刻了这台历史机器，并命名为LisaFPGA，让这台传奇电脑以更强大的形态重获新生。

LisaFPGA的核心是一块基于Xilinx Artix 7-100T FPGA的单板系统，它替代了原版Lisa的CPU和逻辑电路，并集成了2MB SRAM。设计者通过FPGA逻辑精确模拟了Lisa的硬件架构，包括5 MHz Motorola 68000 CPU（可通过物理开关超频至等效75 MHz）、软驱、硬盘、串口以及键盘鼠标接口。创新之处在于，该复刻板完全不需要原版笨重的视频转换器：它原生支持HDMI视频输出，并带有复古的扫描线效果选项，主显示模式（黑白/灰度）可通过开关实时切换。输入设备方面，板卡既保留了原版Lisa的DB9键盘/鼠标接口，又贴心地提供了USB端口，用户无需寻找古董外设即可使用现代键鼠。串口功能则通过内置USB集线器重定向到USB-C接口，避免了老式DB25串口线和USB转串口适配器的麻烦。软盘镜像可以从SD卡加载，或通过直接连接PC获取，甚至支持外接原始软驱。

性能参数上，原版Lisa因软件设计选择（如面向对象操作系统）和5 MHz低频CPU而运行缓慢。Alex为FPGA实现加入了两个超频倍率选项（物理开关选择），使CPU等效频率可提升至75 MHz，大幅提升响应速度。此外，板卡上的SRAM容量是原版（最初为1MB，后期可扩展至2MB）的精确对应。视频输出支持Lisa的两种原生分辨率（720×364和608×432），且原生HDMI接口消除了复刻用户对老式显示器的依赖。

目前发布的版本是LisaFPGA v2，但Alex已预告v3即将到来，会修复若干问题。他表示将在理顺所有事项后，通过GitHub完全开源整个项目（包括硬件设计文件和FPGA代码）。此外，他正考虑销售复刻板卡，并计划在月末的Vintage Computer Festival Southwest上演讲。对复古计算爱好者而言，这个项目不仅是一次精准的硬件复刻，更通过现代接口和超频能力，让Lisa这台具有历史意义的机器重新焕发实用价值：教育展示、软件考古、体验早期GUI操作系统（Lisa Office System 7/7）以及运行当时的商业软件。

值得一提的是，虽然苹果因Mac的普及常被误认为是GUI和鼠标发明者，但真正的发明者另有其人：GUI源自施乐PARC实验室的Xerox Star 8010工作站（1981年，售价16,595美元，约合今天6万美元），而鼠标则早在1963年由斯坦福研究所设计。Lisa的重要贡献在于将这两项技术以相对平易的价格带入个人电脑市场，尽管其命运跌宕，但LisaFPGA的重现，让后人得以亲手触摸这段计算机史的重要章节。
